content.text: where is the pc-switcher config file installed when i do "pc-switcher init"?

input.command: grep -rn "def init\|init(" src/pcswitcher/cli.py 2>/dev/null | head; echo "---"; grep -rn "config" src/pcswitcher/*.py | grep -i "path\|home\|xdg\|\.config" | head -30
input.description: Find init command and config path logic

content.text:
`pc-switcher init` writes the config to:

```
~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml
```

Details from the code (`src/pcswitcher/cli.py:462-489`):

- The path comes from `Configuration.get_default_config_path()` → `Path.home() / ".config" / "pc-switcher" / "config.yaml"` (`config.py:181-183`).
- `init` creates the parent directory (`config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)`) and writes the contents of the packaged `pcswitcher/default-config.yaml` template into it.
- If the file already exists, it refuses unless you pass `--force`.

This is not configurable via a flag on `init` — it always targets the default path. (The `sync` and other commands do accept `--config` to point elsewhere, defaulting to the same `~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml`.)
